inputs.prompt:
ultra-realistic cinematic video inside an authentic Indian tier-2 hardware shop during the afternoon. The shop is filled with hanging electrical wires, PVC pipes, switch boxes, paint buckets, tools, and hardware products. Warm natural sunlight enters from the storefront.

A 6-year-old Indian boy stands at the counter holding a bright orange plastic toy gun. He is enthusiastically playing "Chor-Sipahi." In excitement, he points the toy gun toward the counter and loudly shouts,

Kid (excited, playful): "Dhiishoooom!"

Immediately, the child grabs the small blue-and-white hardware payment soundbox from the counter and holds it to his mouth like a police walkie-talkie. He speaks dramatically with complete confidence:

Kid: "Hello control room... chor bhag gaya!"

The middle-aged Indian shopkeeper, Ramesh, wearing a checked shirt, instantly reacts with comic panic. He quickly but gently reaches across the counter with both hands, trying to pull the soundbox away without hurting the child. His facial expression shows genuine concern mixed with humorous frustration.

As he carefully tugs the soundbox back, he says naturally:

Ramesh (panicking): "Arre beta! Yeh khilona nahi hai, chhod do! Isse dukan ka payment pata chalta hai. Agar toot gaya toh naya dabba khareedna padega!"

The child keeps smiling and continues pretending to be a police officer while lightly resisting.

Camera Direction:

Single continuous handheld shot.
Eye-level perspective.
Begin with a medium shot of the child, then subtly push in as the child grabs the soundbox.
Slight handheld movement during Ramesh's reaction to enhance realism.
End on a close two-shot capturing both the child's playful grin and Ramesh's worried expression.

Style:
Ultra-photorealistic, natural Indian faces, authentic Tier-2 hardware shop, documentary realism, warm afternoon sunlight, realistic body language, synchronized Hindi lip movement, expressive facial acting, subtle motion blur, 35mm lens, shallow depth of field, premium commercial quality, no subtitles, no background music, only natural shop ambience and spoken dialogue.

ultra-realistic cinematic video inside an authentic Indian tier-2 hardware shop during the afternoon. The shop is filled with hanging electrical wires, PVC pipes, switchboards, paint buckets, tools, and hardware products. Warm sunlight streams in from the shop entrance.

On the wooden counter, a blue-and-white hardware payment soundbox is clearly visible, standing upright near the edge of the counter. The soundbox remains untouched at the beginning of the scene.

A 6-year-old Indian boy stands in front of the counter holding a bright orange plastic toy gun in one hand. He is excitedly playing "Chor-Sipahi."

The boy suddenly points the toy gun toward the counter and loudly shouts:

Kid (excited): "Dhiishoom!"

Only after saying "Dhaishoom!", the child notices the payment soundbox sitting on the counter. He quickly reaches forward with his free hand, grabs the soundbox off the counter, lifts it to his mouth like a police walkie-talkie, 
The moment the child picks up the soundbox, Ramesh, a middle-aged Indian shopkeeper in a checked shirt standing behind the counter, immediately reacts with comic panic. (the child picks up the soundbox with his left hand and takes it near his mouth and speaks)He quickly leans forward and gently tries to take the soundbox back without frightening the child. and confidently says :

Kid: "Hello control room... chor bhag gaya!"

While carefully pulling the soundbox, he says:

Ramesh (panicking): "Arre beta! Yeh khilona nahi hai, chhod do! Isse dukan ka payment pata chalta hai. Agar toot gaya toh naya dabba khareedna padega!"

The child keeps smiling, lightly resists, and continues pretending to be a police officer while holding the soundbox.

Camera Blocking
0–3 sec: Medium shot. The soundbox is clearly visible on the counter. Child points the toy gun and says "Dhaishoom!"
3–8 sec: Child holds the soundbox like a walkie-talkie and says, "Hello control room... chor bhag gaya!"
8–15 sec: Ramesh immediately reaches in, gently pulls the soundbox near his mouth and says his dialogue. End on both characters in frame with the child grinning and Ramesh looking worried.
Style

Photorealistic, authentic Indian Tier-2 hardware shop, natural Indian faces, realistic body language, documentary-style handheld camera, 35mm lens, shallow depth of field, synchronized Hindi lip movement, warm afternoon lighting, premium commercial quality, no subtitles, no background music, only natural shop ambience and dialogue. Emphasize clear sequential action: the soundbox starts on the counter, then the child picks it up, then Ramesh reacts.

CUSTOMER: (Laughing) रमेश भैया, ये डब्बा खिलौना ही तो है! बस पेमेंट अलर्ट ही तो देता है, इससे अच्छा अलर्ट पे डाउनलोड करो।

RAMESH(Confused): Alert pay kya hai?

CUSTOMER: "अलर्ट पे एक ऐप है जिससे हर पेमेंट का लाउड और क्लियर अलर्ट सीधा फोन पर ही आ जाता है और इसमें KYC की भी कोई ज़रूरत नहीं।"
